evidence: Supabase compatibility policy is configured as a contract around `@/integrations/supabase/client`, but enforcement depends on `src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseAllowlist.json` rather than scanning current imports.
Current production imports of `@/integrations/supabase/client` are 151 (`rg ... | rg -v '__tests__|.test.|.spec.' | wc -l`), while policy phase budget for March 2026 is 129.

Allowlist contains missing/invalid paths (`src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useCredits.ts`, `src/shared/hoo../../compat/hooks/useExternalApiKeys.ts`), so governance data is stale and not a reliable architecture boundary signal.
No cycle summaries were provided in `holistic_context.dependencies` (both `cycle_summaries` and `existing_cycles` are null), so cycle concerns are unconfirmed in this batch.

Workflow integrity check: no loop-prone reopen/rerun orchestration behavior was found in reviewed pipeline files (`src/shared/lib/tasks/batchTaskPipeline.ts`, `src/shared/lib/taskCreation/batchResults.ts`).
`ProjectContext` was split into selection/crud/identity contexts, but `useProject()` recombines all three into one compatibility object in `src/shared/contexts/ProjectContext.tsx`.
Call-site usage remains dominated by `useProject()` across many modules (tools/pages/shared components), so decomposition intent is not realized and ownership boundaries stay broad.
This compatibility façade pattern keeps role boundaries blurred: consumers needing only one field still import a broad project contract.

Confirmed behavioral outlier: processing-task pagination switches to client-side slicing, but page>1 fetches only `limit` rows without range/offset, then slices by offset, producing empty later pages despite non-zero count (`src/shared/hooks/tasks/paginatedTaskRepository.ts`, used by `src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts`).

Confirmed mixed error strategy in the same segment-settings seam: some query hooks swallow DB errors and return null/false with console logging (`src/shared/hooks/segments/usePairMetadata.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/segments/useShotVideoSettings.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/segments/useSegmentMutations.ts`) while adjacent hooks normalize and rethrow (`src/shared/hooks/segments/useSegmentOutputsForShot.ts`).

Confirmed exception hotspot propagation gap: `submitSegmentTask` always proceeds after `saveSettings()` even when save function reports failure via `false`, so boundary state persistence and task creation can diverge (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/submitSegmentTask.ts` with `src/shared/hooks/segments/useSegmentMutations.ts`).

Confirmed duplicate-cluster seam drift: prompt-shortening logic is duplicated with different heuristics (30-char utility, 20-char prefix check, inline 30-char fallback) across prompt management and task-param construction (`src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/promptUtils.ts`, `.../usePromptHandlers.ts`, `.../buildBatchTaskParams.ts`).

Loop-prone pattern check (workflow integrity): refetch guard can repeatedly re-trigger on stale empty processing queries with no attempt cap/circuit breaker (`src/shared/hooks/tasks/useProcessingRefetchGuard.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/tasks/taskFetchPolicy.ts`).

`src/shared/components/ui/runtime/toastManager.ts` is a pure pass-through wrapper over `src/shared/runtime/toastRuntime.ts` (initialize/get/reset) with no additional policy, creating duplicate import surfaces.

`src/shared/lib/tasks/imageInpaint.ts` and `src/shared/lib/tasks/annotatedImageEdit.ts` duplicate near-identical single-task payload assembly and multi-task fanout logic, but error/result handling diverges (`processBatchResults` vs custom filtering/logging), increasing local drift risk.

`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx` and `useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` each perform large repeated `useMemo` model assembly blocks with broad dependency arrays and similar field wiring, indicating over-delegated local plumbing.

`supabase/functions/complete_task/generation-core.test.ts` only asserts exported symbols are functions, while `generation-core.ts` contains critical DB branching/error behavior used by `generation.ts`, `generation-child.ts`, and `generation-handlers.ts`.

`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/MotionControl.tsx` (508 LOC) and `ShotSettingsContext.tsx` (399 LOC) are only covered by import/module-coverage tests (`src/moduleCoverageConsolidated.test.ts`, `src/moduleImportCoverage.test.ts`) rather than behavior assertions for prop/state transitions and context contracts.

Direct tests for request parsing exist (`supabase/functions/complete_task/request.direct.test.ts`) but cover only two happy/guard paths and miss mode-routing, thumbnail path validation branches, and orchestrator security-lookup failure branches.
Edge JSON error envelopes are inconsistent: `_shared/edgeRequest.ts` uses `{error,errorCode,message,recoverable}` while `_shared/edgeHandler.ts` JSON errors use `{error}` via `buildErrorResponse`.
`complete_task/request.ts` returns plain-text `Response` objects for parse failures, while the rest of edge stack routes through structured JSON envelopes and operation-result contracts.
The same endpoint (`complete_task/index.ts`) mixes shared edge bootstrap contracts with bespoke parse/response shapes, producing caller-visible inconsistency for error handling.

Legacy-structure compatibility logic is duplicated in multiple places: `LEGACY_TRAVEL_STRUCTURE_FIELDS`/`LEGACY_TRAVEL_STRUCTURE_VIDEO_FIELDS` are redefined in `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts` even though `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts` already owns the same migration surface.

`payloadBuilder.ts` throws guidance to "pass explicit legacyStructureCompatibility" (line ~108), but no such field exists on `TravelBetweenImagesTaskInput` in `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts`, creating dead migration guidance.
Migration shims remain as thin pass-through modules (for example `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/InlineSegmentVideo.tsx`) instead of being retired after import migration.
holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles shows `src/shared/components/` has 53 files with low average fan-in (3.3), indicating a broad, low-cohesion catch-all surface.

`src/shared/components/` root mixes UI render components with non-component artifacts (`segmentSettingsMigration.ts`, `segmentSettingsMigrationLegacy.ts`, `segmentSettingsUtils.ts`, `useImageGenerationDetailsViewModel.ts`).

Single-owner files are placed in shared root: `ConstellationCanvas.tsx` is imported by `src/pages/Home/components/hero/HomeBackground.tsx` only; `CreateProjectModal.tsx` is imported by `src/shared/components/GlobalHeader/index.tsx` only.

Batch concern_signals are empty; no packet-provided mechanical concern was confirmed for this batch. scan_evidence complexity hotspots exist but are mostly in other large files and do not counter the placement defects found here.

Confirmed mechanical signal in src/app/bootstrap.tsx: bootstrap file documents a strict import-order contract ('Logger initialization - MUST BE FIRST') and relies on logger side effects before any other runtime setup.

Confirmed initialization coupling across src/app/bootstrap.tsx, src/integrations/supabase/client.ts, and src/integrations/supabase/runtime/supabaseRuntime.ts: many modules call getSupabaseClient() directly while runtime throws if bootstrap did not run first.

Confirmed ambient mutable globals in dev runtime: src/integrations/supabase/debug/initializeSupabaseDebugGlobals.ts, src/shared/realtime/DataFreshnessManager.ts, src/shared/services/network/networkStatusDebug.ts, and src/integrations/supabase/reconnect/ReconnectScheduler.ts assign manager/client instances onto window.* keys.

Refuted mechanical signal relevance for initialization_coupling in src/domains/generation/hooks/useDeleteGenerationAction.ts and src/pages/Home/components/motionComparison/useMotionComparisonController.ts: deeply nested closures are present but do not create import-time side effects or boot-order dependencies.

Refuted mechanical signal relevance for initialization_coupling in src/domains/generation/mappers/generationParamsMapper.ts and src/pages/Home/components/panes/PhilosophyPane.tsx: complexity/size concerns exist, but no module-scope initialization dependency was found.

Refuted stale signal in src/integrations/supabase/bootstrap/createSupabaseClient.ts and src/features/lora/hooks/useHuggingFaceUpload.ts: flagged 'async without await' does not appear as an initialization defect in current code paths.

In src/shared/hooks/segments/useSegmentSettings.ts, the public contract declares saveSettings: () => Promise<boolean>, and src/shared/hooks/segments/useSegmentMutations.ts returns false on multiple persistence failures.
In src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/submitSegmentTask.ts, maybeSaveSettings awaits saveSettings() but ignores the boolean result and proceeds to task creation regardless.
This creates a behavior/signature mismatch where 'save failed' can still produce a submitted task, violating caller expectations for a boolean success contract.

src/shared/components/segmentSettingsMigration.ts uses repeated direct casts from Record<string, unknown> (e.g., prompt/negativePrompt/numFrames/motionMode/phaseConfig) without runtime narrowing, so SegmentSettings can be populated with invalid runtime types while still type-checking.

src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/submitSegmentTask.ts force-casts buildTaskParams(...) from Record<string, unknown> to IndividualTravelSegmentTaskParams via `as unknown as`, bypassing compile-time shape checks.

src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/reposition/useImageTransform.ts casts activeVariantParams?.transform to ImageTransform and then src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/reposition/useCanvasTransform.ts performs numeric math/transform string interpolation on those fields; malformed persisted data yields NaN/invalid transforms at runtime.

src/integrations/supabase/bootstrap/createSupabaseClient.ts mutates client.rest.fetch through `as unknown as { rest: { fetch: ... } }`, coupling behavior to untyped internal shape and defeating type guarantees.

Mechanical signal refutation: the scan's dead_function hotspot on src/integrations/supabase/bootstrap/fetchWithTimeout.ts is not supported here; composeAbortSignals is used by fetchWithTimeout and by fetchWithTimeout tests.

findings.summary: Generation-domain hook is coupled to UI dialog props type.
findings.suggestion: Move dialog contract typing to a domain-agnostic contract module (e.g., `shared/components/dialogs/contracts` or `domains/generation/contracts`) and make the domain hook return a domain-specific confirm-state model. Adapt to UI props at the UI boundary.
evidence: `useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm` imports `DeleteGenerationConfirmDialogProps` from a UI component file and returns `confirmDialogProps` typed to that UI contract.
Multiple feature/tool flows consume this hook as domain behavior, so UI-prop shape changes can ripple through domain and feature code.

findings.summary: Global error facade mixes core reporting with UI presentation side effects.
findings.suggestion: Split the façade into explicit layers: keep `reportError` for core normalization/logging and add a UI-layer adapter (`presentError`) invoked only by UI/application shells. Replace broad direct `normalizeAndPresentError` imports in lower-level modules with pure reporting API.
evidence: `normalizeAndPresentError` always routes through report+presentation path (`notifyError`) based on options/appError state.
Presenter installation happens in app bootstrap (`installErrorNotifier`), creating hidden runtime coupling between core error API behavior and app boot sequence.
With 246 importers from holistic context, this mixed concern becomes a central architectural dependency.

findings.summary: Supabase readiness is tracked by two separate module states.
findings.suggestion: Use a single source of truth for readiness (runtime singleton state) and remove `hasInitializedRuntimeClient`. In `getSupabaseClientResult`, derive readiness from runtime module state only.
evidence: `client.ts` uses `hasInitializedRuntimeClient` gate, while runtime module owns actual `runtimeClient` singleton.
Accessor path can fail before checking runtime singleton if boolean gate is false, so readiness truth is split across files.
Bootstrap initializes via `initializeSupabase()`, but exported runtime functions still expose alternate paths that can diverge from the gate model.

findings.summary: Compatibility hook preserves monolithic project dependency across the app.
findings.suggestion: Deprecate `useProject()` and migrate consumers to `useProjectSelectionContext`, `useProjectCrudContext`, or `useProjectIdentityContext` based on need. Keep a temporary shim only for legacy callers and track remaining imports to zero.
evidence: `ProjectContext.tsx` exports fine-grained hooks, but also `useProject()` that merges CRUD, selection, and identity into one object.
Many consumers still call `useProject()` rather than narrow hooks, keeping broad coupling and reducing boundary clarity.
This creates a persistent 'god context' access pattern despite internal provider splitting.

findings.summary: Processing-task pagination can silently drop page>1 results due to mismatched fetch/slice strategy.
findings.suggestion: Unify pagination contract for processing filters: either fetch enough rows for requested offset (`limit * page` with bounded cap) before slicing, or move processing-priority ordering into SQL and keep server-side `range` pagination. Add a regression test for page 2+ under processing filter.
evidence: When `isProcessingStatusFilter` is true, data query uses `.limit(fetchLimit)` instead of `.range(offset, ...)`.
For `page > 1`, `fetchLimit` becomes base limit, but response still does `sortProcessingTasks(...).slice(offset, offset + limit)`.
Example: page 2 with limit 50 fetches 50 rows then slices `[50,100)`, yielding an empty page despite possible additional matching tasks.

findings.summary: Segment task submission ignores settings-save failure and still enqueues generation tasks.
findings.suggestion: Make `saveSettings` part of an explicit Result contract (`{ ok:boolean; reason?:... }`) and gate task creation on `ok`. Surface a user-visible error and stop submission when persistence fails.
evidence: `saveSettings` is typed as `() => Promise<boolean | void>`, and mutation hooks return `false` on persistence failures.
`maybeSaveSettings` only awaits `saveSettings()` but does not inspect result or abort submission.
Both regenerate/slot forms pass this save function into `submitSegmentTask`, so failed metadata persistence can be followed by task creation using unsaved state.

findings.summary: Adjacent segment hooks expose inconsistent failure semantics (null/false vs throw), making orchestration brittle.
findings.suggestion: Standardize segment data/mutation hooks on one boundary error contract (either throw typed errors for query states, or return explicit `Result` objects everywhere) and update consumers to branch consistently on that contract.
evidence: `usePairMetadata` and `useShotVideoSettings` log and return `null` on query errors.
`useSegmentMutations` logs and returns `false` for failure paths.
`useSegmentOutputsForShot` normalizes and rethrows query errors, producing a materially different upstream handling path in the same feature seam.

findings.summary: Prompt short-label generation rules are duplicated with mismatched heuristics across prompt handoff points.
findings.suggestion: Centralize a single short-label policy (generation + overwrite-detection comparator) in one utility and consume it from handlers, submission transforms, and batch param builder to remove divergent behavior.
evidence: Canonical helper `toShortPrompt` truncates to 30 chars.
`usePromptHandlers` decides auto-overwrite by checking `startsWith(prompt.fullPrompt.substring(0, 20))`, diverging from 30-char policy.
`buildBatchTaskParams` reimplements inline 30-char + ellipsis fallback instead of using the same helper used by submission transforms.

findings.summary: Lightbox split introduced adapter-heavy seams that remap the same state repeatedly.
findings.suggestion: Collapse to a single typed lightbox domain model with stable selectors: keep one normalization step at boundary entry, then pass domain objects directly to image/video render layers. Remove alias facades that only rename existing models and extract shared render-model composition helpers used by both branches.
evidence: `buildImageSharedLightboxInput` in `ImageLightbox.tsx` maps many fields into grouped props, then downstream render-model builders (`buildVariantsModel`, `buildWorkflowBarModel`, `buildWorkflowControlsModel`) remap overlapping data again.

`useSharedLightboxState.ts` adds layered facades and alias exports around already grouped state (`useSharedVariantsState` -> `useSharedLightboxInteractionState` -> `useLightboxPanelModel`), increasing delegation density.
Video and image lightbox branches both reconstruct similar large view-model objects separately, so the abstraction boundary did not remove cross-branch choreography.

findings.summary: Sibling task modules use incompatible creation pipelines, weakening shared contracts.
findings.suggestion: Standardize all task creators on `runTaskCreationPipeline` + `composeTaskRequest`; keep direct `createTask` only for explicitly documented exceptions. Add a lightweight shared helper for single/multi task fanout so batch behavior and error contracts stay uniform.
evidence: `taskCreatorPipeline.ts` defines a shared validation/request/error path, but multiple task modules bypass it and call `createTask` directly.

`imageGeneration.ts`, `joinClips.ts`, `magicEdit.ts`, `videoEnhance.ts`, `individualTravelSegment.ts` use the pipeline pattern, while `zImageTurboI2I.ts`, `imageInpaint.ts`, `annotatedImageEdit.ts`, `imageUpscale.ts` do not.
This split creates inconsistent seam behavior for validation, error normalization, and extension points (`onCreated`), forcing callers to remember module-specific semantics.

findings.summary: Annotated edit and inpaint builders duplicate nearly the same local logic with drift.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared masked-edit task builder + shared batch fanout utility (task type as parameter) and make both modules thin wrappers over that primitive.
evidence: Both files define parallel `createSingle*Task` functions assembling almost identical payloads (`image_url`, `mask_url`, lineage fields, optional `loras`, hires fix, model), then parallel `Promise.allSettled` fanout.

Batch error handling differs despite identical flow (`processBatchResults` utility in `imageInpaint.ts` vs bespoke success/failure filtering and logging in `annotatedImageEdit.ts`), which increases drift probability for future bug fixes.

findings.summary: UI toast manager file is a no-op indirection layer over runtime toast singleton.
findings.suggestion: Remove the pass-through module and import `toastRuntime` directly, or make `toastManager.ts` the single canonical owner and stop exporting runtime-level functions from two locations.
evidence: `toastManager.ts` only re-exports initialize/get/reset with renamed imports and no extra policy or typing.
Both modules expose effectively the same API surface, creating duplicate dependency entry points for the same singleton.

findings.summary: Critical complete-task generation primitives are effectively untested beyond export smoke checks
findings.suggestion: Replace export-smoke tests with table-driven behavior tests using stubbed Supabase responses for each branch: no-row vs error in `findExistingGeneration`, insert/variant failures, and `linkGenerationToShot` RPC error swallowing.
evidence: `generation-core.test.ts` only verifies functions exist; no assertions cover DB error/no-row handling, variant insert failures, or shot-link RPC failure behavior.

`generation-core.ts` functions are invoked across the complete_task routing path (`generation.ts`, `generation-handlers.ts`, `generation-child.ts`) where incorrect behavior affects task completion and generation lineage.

findings.summary: Large UI contract modules have import coverage but little direct behavioral verification
findings.suggestion: Add focused component/context tests: verify `MotionControl` mode/preset callbacks and `ShotSettingsContext` domain hooks return coherent slices; keep import coverage tests but stop treating them as risk coverage for these modules.
evidence: Coverage references for these modules are import-only in module coverage tests, not scenario assertions.

`ShotEditor/index.test.tsx` mocks `ShotSettingsContext` instead of validating real context hook contracts, and there is no direct test suite for `MotionControl` state transitions (preset selection/custom switch/mode transitions).

findings.summary: Shared edge APIs expose multiple incompatible error envelope shapes
findings.suggestion: Standardize all edge failures on one shared envelope builder (prefer `edgeErrorResponse`) and remove ad-hoc text/`{error}` responses from edge handler and endpoint-local parsers.
evidence: `edgeRequest.edgeErrorResponse` emits `{ error, errorCode, message, recoverable }`.
`edgeHandler.buildErrorResponse` emits `{ error }` for JSON format.
`complete_task/request.ts` parse failures return plain-text responses, creating a third contract shape.

findings.summary: complete_task uses split orchestration seams instead of one coherent request pipeline
findings.suggestion: Move complete-task parsing to shared bootstrap extension points (or expose a typed parser hook in `_shared/edgeHandler.ts`) so auth/method/body/error mapping remains in one integration seam.
evidence: `complete_task/index.ts` bootstraps via shared handler with `parseBody: "none"`, then performs separate local parse/validation and returns endpoint-specific response shapes.
This duplicates boundary translation responsibilities and increases drift between shared bootstrap behavior and endpoint-local parsing behavior.

findings.summary: Legacy travel-structure field registry is duplicated across migration modules.
findings.suggestion: Create one canonical exported legacy-field registry/helper in `legacyStructureVideo.ts` and import it from `payloadBuilder.ts`; also either add a typed `legacyStructureCompatibility` gate or remove that guidance from the thrown error to match the real contract.
evidence: `payloadBuilder.ts` defines `LEGACY_TRAVEL_STRUCTURE_FIELDS` and `LEGACY_TRAVEL_STRUCTURE_VIDEO_FIELDS` locally, while `legacyStructureVideo.ts` defines and uses the same legacy-field set for policy/enforcement.
`payloadBuilder.ts` error text references `legacyStructureCompatibility`, but `TravelBetweenImagesTaskInput` has no such field in `types.ts`, so migration guidance and typed contract are out of sync.

findings.summary: Compatibility re-export remains in active path instead of finishing import migration.
findings.suggestion: Update timeline consumers to import `InlineSegmentVideo` from the shared component directly, then delete the local shim file and remove any remaining references.
evidence: `Timeline/InlineSegmentVideo.tsx` is a pure re-export shim with no behavior.
`SegmentOutputStrip.tsx` still imports from `./InlineSegmentVideo`, so the shim remains in runtime path instead of importing `@/shared/components/InlineSegmentVideo` directly.

findings.summary: Shot editor controller remains monolithic with high local indirection despite hook extraction.
findings.suggestion: Split the controller into boundary-specific adapters (e.g., `useShotEditorGenerationAdapter`, `useShotEditorMediaAdapter`, `useShotEditorLayoutAdapter`) with narrower interfaces, then shrink `ShotEditorProps` to only boundary inputs owned by the page.
evidence: `useShotEditorController.ts` manages setup, editing, generation, joining, bridge refs, and layout assembly in one 800+ LOC hook, then repackages state through many memoized object bundles before returning `layoutProps`.
`ShotEditorProps` in `state/types.ts` is very wide and mixed-responsibility, driving the controller to accept and forward many optional callbacks/refs across layers.

findings.summary: GenerationRow contract is split between barrel export and view-model type, creating migration drift.
findings.suggestion: Unify on a single canonical exported `GenerationRow` name (or explicitly separate `PersistedGenerationRow` vs `GenerationViewRow` at the barrel), then update imports so each call site uses the intended contract explicitly.
evidence: `index.ts` exports `GenerationRow` from `generationRow.ts` (persisted shape), while `generationViewRow.ts` defines a richer `GenerationRow` including derived view fields (`imageUrl`, `thumbUrl`, badge fields).
Call sites such as `useShotImages.ts` import `GenerationRow` from the barrel but use view-only fields like `g.imageUrl`, indicating contract drift and mixed migration states.

findings.summary: src/shared/components root is overloaded with mixed concerns and weak ownership boundaries
findings.suggestion: Run a staged reorg: (1) create target folders `src/shared/components/project/`, `src/shared/components/prompt-editor/`, `src/shared/components/image-generation/`, `src/shared/components/segment-settings/`; (2) move files in low-risk order with `git mv` (start with files with 1 importer like `CreateProjectModal.tsx`, then view-model/migration utilities, then coupled UI pieces); (3) update imports via `rg -l "@/shared/components/(CreateProjectModal|BulkEditControls|useImageGenerationDetailsViewModel|segmentSettings)" src | xargs sed -i '' ...`; (4) validate with `pnpm typecheck` and focused tests `pnpm vitest src/shared/components/__tests__/BulkEditControls.test.tsx src/shared/components/CreateProjectModal.test.tsx`.
evidence: `src/shared/components/` contains 53 production files (directory_profiles) with avg fan-in 3.3, a signature of low-cohesion placement.
The same root contains both UI elements and migration/view-model files, so folder naming no longer reflects responsibility.
Feature-local components (BulkEdit/CreateProject) are kept at root instead of feature folders, flattening boundaries.

findings.summary: Home and GlobalHeader-owned components are misplaced in shared root
findings.suggestion: Use a two-step move plan: (1) move `ConstellationCanvas.tsx` to `src/pages/Home/components/hero/ConstellationCanvas.tsx` and `CreateProjectModal.tsx` to `src/shared/components/GlobalHeader/CreateProjectModal.tsx`; (2) update import paths in `HomeBackground.tsx` and `GlobalHeader/index.tsx`; (3) run `pnpm typecheck` and component tests for both files. If external reuse is later needed, re-export intentionally from a feature index instead of placing directly in shared root.
evidence: `HomeBackground.tsx` imports `ConstellationCanvas` from shared root, and no other production importer appears.
`GlobalHeader/index.tsx` imports `CreateProjectModal` from shared root, and no other production importer appears.
These are >60% affinity (effectively 100%) to one owning area, but current placement advertises them as general-purpose shared UI.

findings.summary: Feature-specific reference/segment utilities are surfaced as generic shared APIs
findings.suggestion: Refactor by capability boundary: (1) move `useHydratedReferences.ts` under `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/referenceManagement/` (or a new `src/domains/references/` package if reused cross-feature); (2) move `galleryUtils.ts` function into a domain file like `src/shared/lib/segments/extractSegmentImages.ts`; (3) add transitional re-exports for one release cycle to avoid large PR blast radius; (4) update imports and validate with `pnpm typecheck` plus targeted tests (`useHydratedReferences`, `galleryUtils`, `useSourceImageChanges`, `useVideoRegenerateMode`).
evidence: `useHydratedReferences` is consumed by `ImageGenerationForm` flow (`useProjectImageSettings`) but lives in top-level shared hooks.
`extractSegmentImages` in `galleryUtils.ts` is used by source-mismatch and video-regenerate logic, not by a broad gallery utility surface.
Generic names/locations hide actual ownership (references domain and segment-regeneration domain), making change boundaries less predictable.

findings.summary: Clipboard behavior is split across multiple unrelated layers without a single owner
findings.suggestion: Create one boundary module (for example `src/shared/platform/clipboard/`) with `copyText` + optional React hook wrappers; migrate in stages: (1) adopt boundary in `SettingsModal/commandUtils.ts` and `TaskDetails/VideoTravelDetails.tsx`; (2) migrate direct callers found by `rg "navigator\.clipboard\.writeText" src/shared src/tools`; (3) keep `useCopyToClipboard` as a thin wrapper over the boundary; (4) run `pnpm typecheck` and clipboard-related tests.
evidence: A safe clipboard helper exists in `src/shared/lib/clipboard.ts`, but many components still call `navigator.clipboard.writeText` directly.
A parallel hook abstraction exists (`useCopyToClipboard`) plus another safe-copy implementation in SettingsModal utilities.
This duplicates policy/location decisions (silent-fail, feedback, fallback behavior) and makes ownership of clipboard contracts unclear.

findings.summary: Join Clips handoff is coupled through a single unscoped mutable global queue (`localStorage.pendingJoinClips`), allowing cross-project/session contamination and non-deterministic startup behavior.
findings.suggestion: Replace the global key queue with a scoped registry (`pendingJoinClips:<projectId>:<userId>`) or move handoff to a typed in-app store/query cache keyed by project. Make producer and consumer share one typed contract module and require `projectId` at write/consume boundaries.
evidence: `useJoinClips` appends payloads to hard-coded key `pendingJoinClips` without project/user scope (`localStorage.setItem('pendingJoinClips', ...)`).
`consumePendingJoinClips` reads from the same global key and clears it (`localStorage.removeItem(PENDING_CLIPS_KEY)`), with no projectId filter in payload schema.

`useClipManager` consumes this queue independently of project identity (`useEffect` depends on `settingsLoaded` only), so queue consumption is not tied to explicit route/project initialization contracts.

findings.summary: Join Clips can overwrite persisted clip settings with empty state before hydration completes due to effect ordering and missing hydration gate.
findings.suggestion: Add an explicit hydration phase flag and block persistence until initial settings hydration (and preload path) completes. Alternatively collapse hydrate+persist into a single reducer/state machine with explicit `UNINITIALIZED -> HYDRATED -> DIRTY` transitions.
evidence: `useClipManager` starts with `clips=[]` and has a persist effect that runs whenever `settingsLoaded` is true (`lines 146-165`), serializing and calling `joinSettings.updateFields` if different.

Hydration effect (`lines 111-141`) may set clips asynchronously after poster preloading; during that window persist effect can compare existing saved clips against empty `clipsToSave` and write empty values.
No guard like `hasHydratedInitialState`/`isHydrating` is checked in the persist effect before writing.

findings.summary: Pending Join Clips queue can be consumed and cleared before target project binding stabilizes, causing dropped queued work on project transitions.
findings.suggestion: Gate queue consumption on a stable `selectedProjectId` and include projectId in queue entries. Only clear consumed entries after they are bound to the same project context and merged successfully; keep unmatched entries for later drain.
evidence: Queue consume effect runs on `[settingsLoaded]` only (`useClipManager` lines 90-106), not on `selectedProjectId`.

Project change reset effect (`lines 76-85`) clears in-memory clips; consumed queue entries are not replayable because `consumePendingJoinClips` removes storage key once read (`clipInitService` line 272).
This creates a workflow blind spot: packet/work item (pending clip action) is generated and then dropped if consumption happens before the final project context is settled.

findings.summary: Supabase compatibility policy is enforced from stale allowlist data, not real imports.
findings.suggestion: Replace allowlist-count gating with generated import-graph gating in CI: (1) scan production TS/TSX imports of `@/integrations/supabase/client`, (2) fail if count exceeds `getLegacySupabaseImportBudget(new Date())`, (3) optionally regenerate allowlist from scanner output and reject missing paths. Keep allowlist only as a review artifact, not enforcement source.
evidence: Budget assertion in test compares `allowlist.files.length` to `getLegacySupabaseImportBudget()`.
Actual production importers of `@/integrations/supabase/client` are 151, above March budget 129.
Allowlist has invalid/nonexistent entries, so passing budget checks does not guarantee runtime conformance.

findings.summary: Runtime-error alias governance tests verify constants but not real importer budget compliance.
findings.suggestion: Add a governance contract test that scans production imports for the runtime-error alias target and enforces `getRuntimeErrorAliasImportBudget(now)`; fail on missing files and on over-budget counts. Reuse the same scanner helper introduced for Supabase to avoid divergent gate logic.
evidence: Current tests assert owner/removeBy/phases and date-to-budget mapping only.
No test computes importers for runtime-error alias usage and compares to budget phases.
This leaves compatibility policy unenforced even though policy metadata exists.

findings.summary: UI button 'contract' path is not the runtime canonical path, creating contract-vs-runtime drift.
findings.suggestion: Choose one canonical contract path and enforce it: either (a) make `ui/button.tsx` the only contract and remove `contracts/button.tsx`, or (b) route all production imports to `contracts/button` and make it re-export the themed runtime button. Add an import-lint rule to block non-canonical entrypoints.
evidence: `contracts/button.tsx` exports `BaseButton`/`baseButtonVariants` as a contract facade.
Production imports target `@/shared/components/ui/button`, while `contracts/button` is referenced only in contract tests.
Two different button surfaces (base primitive vs themed app button) under 'contract' naming blur ownership and expected compatibility boundary.

findings.summary: Package layout mixes many concerns in `shared/components` and keeps thin contract wrappers detached from usage.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) create target folders `src/features/<domain>/components` for domain-specific components and keep only true primitives in `src/shared/components/ui`; 2) move files from `src/shared/components/` by concern (task panes, media/lightbox, onboarding/settings) in small batches; 3) collapse `ui/contracts/` into a single canonical contract entrypoint policy; 4) update imports with `rg -l "@/shared/components/..." | xargs` codemod or ts-morph script; 5) validate with `pnpm vitest` and `pnpm tsc --noEmit`; 6) add lint rule forbidding new domain components under `src/shared/components/` except approved primitives.
evidence: `src/shared/components/` has 53 production files with mixed domains and utilities per directory profile.
`src/shared/components/ui/contracts/` is a tiny wrapper area with very high fan-in and inconsistent runtime usage.
Coupling matrix shows heavy one-way dependency concentration into shared UI from multiple feature directories, increasing blast radius for package-level changes.

findings.summary: Logging behavior depends on import order because logger mutates global console at module load time.
findings.suggestion: Move console interception and unload wiring behind an explicit initializeLoggerRuntime() called from bootstrap; keep module import side-effect-free and idempotent.
evidence: bootstrap.tsx requires logger import to be first and calls this out as mandatory for runtime correctness.
logger.ts executes initializeConsole() at module scope and also installs window.beforeunload listener during import, creating side effects outside explicit bootstrap control.

findings.summary: Supabase access is ambient and bootstrap-dependent, coupling many hooks/services to a hidden global init sequence.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a typed runtime registry/context (or provider) that owns Supabase lifecycle and inject client into services/hooks; reserve throwing accessor for legacy adapters only and migrate call sites incrementally.
evidence: client.ts throws when getSupabaseClient() is called before initializeSupabase(), encoding a hard runtime ordering dependency.
Many domain/service modules import getSupabaseClient as supabase and call supabase() directly, so these modules implicitly require app bootstrap to have executed.
bootstrap.tsx performs initializeSupabase() as side-effectful environment setup, making correctness dependent on app entrypoint order rather than explicit dependency injection.

findings.summary: Dev diagnostics expose multiple mutable window globals without a single registry, creating last-writer-wins and stale-reference risk during long-lived dev sessions/HMR.
findings.suggestion: Centralize dev global exposure in one debug registry module with versioned keys, duplicate-registration guards, and teardown hooks for HMR/test isolation.
evidence: Separate modules assign different manager/client instances directly onto window (__supabase_client__, __DATA_FRESHNESS_MANAGER__, __NETWORK_STATUS_MANAGER__, __RECONNECT_SCHEDULER__).
No shared registration/cleanup contract exists, so repeated initialization paths can overwrite globals without lifecycle validation.

findings.summary: Segment submission ignores saveSettings success/failure contract before creating tasks.
findings.suggestion: Make submit flow branch on save result: require saveSettings to return Promise<boolean>, abort task submission when false, and surface an explicit user-facing error. Remove `| void` from SubmitSegmentTaskInput once all callers comply.
evidence: submitSegmentTask.ts defines saveSettings as Promise<boolean | void>, and maybeSaveSettings only awaits it without checking return value.
useSegmentSettings.ts exposes saveSettings: () => Promise<boolean>.
useSegmentMutations.ts save paths return false on fetch/update exceptions and error branches, so failure is explicitly encoded but dropped by submit path.

findings.summary: Segment settings/task params rely on unsafe unknown-to-domain casts instead of validated decoding.
findings.suggestion: Introduce explicit runtime decoders/guards for metadata/task-param parsing and change buildTaskParams to return a concrete typed task-params interface. Remove `as unknown as` by making shape compatibility compile-time checked end-to-end.
evidence: extractSettingsFromParams in segmentSettingsMigration.ts casts unknown values directly to string/number/boolean/PhaseConfig unions.
buildTaskParams in segmentSettingsUtils.ts returns Record<string, unknown>, forcing consumers to recover domain type later.
submitSegmentTask.ts performs `params as unknown as IndividualTravelSegmentTaskParams`, suppressing mismatch detection between builder output and task contract.

findings.summary: Persisted transform payload is trusted without validation and used as numeric math input.
findings.suggestion: Add a strict `parseImageTransform` guard that validates each field (finite numbers + boolean flags + bounds where relevant) and falls back to DEFAULT_TRANSFORM on invalid input before state assignment.
evidence: useImageTransform.ts reads `activeVariantParams?.transform as ImageTransform | undefined` from Record<string, unknown> and only checks object-ness, not field types/ranges.
useCanvasTransform.ts consumes transform fields in arithmetic and CSS transform composition (`translate`, `scale`, `rotate`) assuming valid numbers/booleans.

findings.summary: Supabase client behavior depends on a double-cast mutation of an internal property.
findings.suggestion: Wrap request customization behind supported Supabase extension points (or a local typed adapter/factory) and isolate one narrowly-typed boundary for the patch with runtime assertion + version guard if internal mutation remains unavoidable.
evidence: createSupabaseClient.ts sets `(client as unknown as { rest: { fetch: typeof dataFetch } }).rest.fetch = dataFetch`.
This bypasses official client typings and ties correctness to an undocumented internal shape; compiler cannot detect drift in Supabase internals.
